reasoning: HB 831 and all its companion bills regarding proof of citizenship for voter registration were indefinitely postponed/withdrawn and died in committee as of May 3, 2025. While the Florida legislative session was extended from May 2 to June 6, 2025, this extension is primarily to complete the state budget. In Florida, extended sessions are heavily restricted in scope, focusing almost entirely on the budget and conforming bills. Reviving a dead, non-budget policy issue like voter citizenship verification as a late amendment would be highly irregular and procedurally difficult. Given that leadership allowed the primary bills to die in committee, they clearly do not prioritize passing this measure in the current session. Thus, it is extremely unlikely that such legislation will be enacted before July 1, 2025.
